metadata.description: Praxis — an enterprise-grade quality harness and autonomous task engine for Claude Code. Routes every prompt (not just slash commands) into the full pipeline: restructure into a spec, plan, implement to production standard with the right engineering best-practices, then audit adversarially (regression/duplication/performance/edge-case/completeness/doc-first, plus accessibility and design-consistency on UI changes). Runs a front-end pipeline with real design craft (research → wireframes → design system → build → optimize) for any UI niche, and keeps the project's living knowledge (/docs, CHANGELOG, ADRs) current with no regression. Deterministic gates block unfinished work and verify the tests actually passed.

plugins.name: praxis
plugins.description: Turn any prompt — command or not — into a complete, production-ready change with living documentation: restructure → investigate → plan → implement (code-craft + best-practices) → full audit → update /docs, CHANGELOG and ADRs → structured report, then an optional reviewable PR. Bootstraps any repo, keeps CLAUDE.md and docs regression-free, runs fully autonomous in auto-pilot, and gates every change so nothing is placeholdered, deferred, undocumented, or silently out of scope.
